scope: four parent Loop Lab card candidates
criteria_scale: 1-5, maximize; visual_hierarchy is the primary criterion

independent_checks_performed: Re-parsed all four SVGs with xml.etree.ElementTree (all well-formed, single <svg> root, viewBox present, no external href/script/@import).
Recomputed every WCAG contrast ratio from raw hex myself rather than trusting reported values.
Re-derived generative-02 title width using the same 0.55 glyph-metric method the editorial track documented, to test the 'no overlap' claim.

independent_findings: Editorial contrast claims reproduce exactly: ink.muted #A39C8C = 2.49:1 (fail) in loop-01; repaired to #6B6558 = 5.28:1 (pass) in loop-02; accent #B5432E = 5.03:1 (pass).
Editorial-02 layout-metrics labels the format-badge (white on accent) as 5.03:1; the true value is 5.52:1 — a harmless mislabel that still passes.

GENERATIVE CTA CONTRAST IS MISREPORTED in BOTH generative loops: files claim cta_text_bg 4.5, but white on #3b82f6 is actually 3.68:1 — fails the 4.5:1 normal-text AA threshold and clears only the 3:1 large-text/bold exception.

Generative-02 title 'Routing Systems' reaches x~674 under the 0.55 glyph method while its graphic panel begins at x=650 (~24px overshoot); layout-metrics under-reported title width as 550, so the 'no bounding box overlaps' claim is not robust.

editorial-01.rationale: A true editorial artifact — print frame, LL monogram, terracotta kicker, ruled three-column metadata, serif/sans pairing — with an unambiguous title-first reading order and a genuinely structured token system. But it ships a hard, self-acknowledged contrast defect (muted text at 2.49:1) that erases the secondary tier's legibility, and tokens.json encodes only one hardcoded event, so reuse is asserted rather than shown.
top_defects: Blocking contrast failure: #A39C8C at 2.49:1 across 5 nodes (labels, edition tag, microcopy) — independently verified.
Bottom-center rhythm mark is off-grid and reads as a loose ornament.
editorial-01.next_step: Darken the muted role to clear 4.5:1 (#6B6558 -> 5.28:1 is correct) and encode a second event/variant in tokens.json to demonstrate reuse.

layout_quality_defects: ink.muted #A39C8C on paper = 2.49:1, fails WCAG AA normal (4.5) and large (3.0); trips inaccessible-contrast/unreadable-text gate across 5 text nodes

editorial-02.rationale: The loop-01 skeleton kept intact with three surgical changes: muted role recolored to #6B6558 (verified 5.28:1), a subordinate terracotta HYBRID SESSION badge, and new content. It is the only candidate that closes a full build->measure->repair->prove-reuse cycle: the token-level fix repairs five nodes in one edit, and tokens.json now carries a changelog, an optional formatBadge component, and an eventVariants block with two real configurations (in-person vs hybrid) on one grid. Hierarchy is the cleanest of the four and every field is present AND legible. Held off a perfect polish score by a brittle fixed-width badge and a system-font dependency with no embedding fallback.
top_defects: Format badge fixed at 190px for a 14-char label; longer format strings would overflow (own review admits this).
Minor metrics mislabel: badge contrast reported 5.03 vs actual 5.52 (both pass).
One-word second title line leaves the title block slightly unbalanced against full-width rules.
editorial-02.next_step: Make the badge content-driven width (or publish a character budget) and stress-test a long-title + badge collision; otherwise production-ready.

generative-01.rationale: A deliberately-unfinished baseline: phase-shifted signal paths are the most on-brief interpretation of 'modular loops / signal paths' and give real character, but they sweep straight through the metadata band (the source literally comments the collision as a 'Defect'), so the secondary tier fights a decorative layer for the same pixels. tokens.json is a palette, not a system — colors and fonts only, no scale/spacing/components/variants. Metadata icon chips are empty squares.
top_defects: Blocking: signal paths overlap live metadata text (self-admitted), reducing legibility.
Misreported CTA contrast: claimed 4.5:1, actual 3.68:1 (independently recomputed).
Hollow token system + content-free icon chips.
generative-01.next_step: Zone text vs graphic to remove the overlap AND grow tokens.json into a real system with type scale, spacing, and a variant mechanism.

layout_quality_defects: Metadata overlaps decorative signal paths (self-admitted overlap/unreadable gate).
CTA white on #3b82f6 = 3.68:1, misreported as 4.5:1; fails normal-text AA (passes only large-text 3:1 exception).

generative-02.rationale: The strongest single image in the set: a text/graphic hemisphere split where the modular-loop panel reads as an instrument/routing UI — bold, specific, ownable, and the clear winner on raw visual distinctiveness. Zoning fixes loop-01's overlap and the reading order is clean. But the system layer is the weakest of all four: tokens.json is still just colors + fonts (+ badge colors), with no type scale, no spacing, no component contract, and no variant mechanism — so the brief's central reuse requirement is unproven. The CTA contrast is misreported, the widest title line grazes/overshoots the graphic panel by ~24px under the documented method, the icon chips are empty, and the self-review ('5/5, no flaws' / 'Perfect') is uncritical and contradicted by measurement.
top_defects: No variant/system layer: tokens.json cannot support event variants; reuse unproven (blocking for the brief's intent).
Misreported CTA contrast: claimed 4.5:1, actual 3.68:1 (passes only via large-text exception).
Title 'Routing Systems' grazes/overshoots the graphic panel edge (~x674 vs panel x650); empty metadata icon chips.
generative-02.next_step: Keep the visual language; rebuild tokens.json into a real system (scale, spacing, component contracts, explicit variant mechanism), add a real title/panel gutter, give icon chips glyphs, and honestly re-measure/repair the CTA to clear 4.5:1.

layout_quality_risk_flags: CTA white on #3b82f6 = 3.68:1 (misreported as 4.5); passes only under the large-text/bold 3:1 exception, not normal-text AA.
Title/panel margin is negative-to-zero under the editorial track's own 0.55 glyph method (~24px overshoot); metrics under-reported title width as 550, so 'no overlaps' is not robust.

ranking_rationale: editorial-02 leads on the totality of the brief: primary criterion (hierarchy) strongest, all objective gates independently pass, and it is the only candidate that demonstrably proves tokens support variants. generative-02 is second on the strength of the best visual language in the set and a clean reading order, but its token layer does not constitute a reusable system and its self-metrics contain a real contrast error. editorial-01 is third — a strong system undermined by a shipped contrast failure and single-event scope. generative-01 is last: a self-admitted overlap defect, a misreported CTA contrast, and a hollow token file.
preferred_candidate_reason: It is the only artifact that couples strong visual hierarchy with a token system that genuinely supports variants and passes all objective gates under my own independent verification.
dissent: I register explicit, preserved dissent rather than converging on a single 'best in every dimension' verdict. (1) On pure visual/brand distinctiveness, generative-02 is the strongest artifact in the set and I score it 5 — higher than either editorial card; if this deliverable were judged on visual impact alone, generative-02 would win, and I dissent from any consensus that dismisses it purely on token thinness. (2) I dissent sharply from the generative track's self-assessment: 'Perfect' / '5/5, no flaws' is contradicted by my measurements — the CTA contrast is 3.68:1, not the claimed 4.5:1, and the title grazes the graphic panel. I decline to launder those self-reported numbers. (3) My editorial-02 preference is a whole-brief judgment (reuse + gates + hierarchy), not a claim that it is the most beautiful card; on aesthetics I hold that generative-02 is superior, and I keep both opinions in tension deliberately. (4) I am judging only; I promote no champion.
notes: svg_validity and layout_quality are objective pass/fail gates; the 1-5 criteria are subjective and maximized. No other judge's files were read or modified. All contrast and geometry figures above were recomputed independently, not copied from the candidates' metrics.
